title: atriplex garrettii var. navajoensis
intro: Atriplex garrettii var. navajoensis, also known as Navajo saltbrush, is a shrub species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the amaranth family and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in traditional herbal medicine to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Navajo saltbrush include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin.
